parameters.content:
## Alephant — Margin-Aware Paid Agent (x402)

### How it works

A buyer's agent calls your endpoint and pays per request in USDC over **x402**. Alephant verifies and settles that payment first, then forwards the request into this workflow, so revenue is booked before any work runs. The workflow checks your budget, an AI agent reads your recent spend and picks the cheapest model that still does the job, runs the work through Alephant with full cost tracking, calculates net margin on that single call, replies to the buyer, posts the margin to Discord, and throttles itself if profit drops too low.

### Setup steps

1. Install the Alephant community nodes
2. Create an Alephant Virtual Key credential (docs link below)
3. Create an OpenAI credential with Base URL = https://ai.alephant.io/v1 and your Alephant virtual key as the API key, then select it on the 'Agent Brain' node
4. Connect a Discord Webhook credential on the 'Margin Feed' node
5. Edit 'Per-Call P&L' - set your real price, token rate, and fee
6. Run 'Budget Guard' once, then map the real budget field in the 'Budget OK?' node

**Requirements**
- Alephant account (free tier works) - https://alephant.io
- Discord channel webhook

Docs: https://developers.alephant.io/

## 1. Take payment and check budget

x402 settles the buyer's USDC payment before the request arrives here. The workflow then confirms there is budget left before doing any paid work. If the budget is gone, it returns a 402 and stops.

## 2. Agent picks the cheapest model that fits

The AI agent reads recent spend through the Alephant UsageSummary tool, then chooses a model tier that protects margin. Its own reasoning is routed through Alephant too (the 'Agent Brain' base_url), so the auditor shows up in its own dashboard.

## 3. Run the work and calculate profit

Runs the chosen model through Alephant for full cost tracking, then computes revenue minus token spend minus fee = net margin for this single call.

## 4. Deliver, report, and protect

Sends the result to the buyer, posts a live margin line to Discord, and if margin drops below target it calls back to tighten policy and throttle - so a bad call never quietly loses money.

## Who's it for

Developers and n8n builders who sell AI services or paid agent endpoints and want each call to stay profitable. x402 brings the money in per request, the agent keeps model cost down, and every sale gets a profit receipt that self-protects when margin gets thin.

={{ 'Buyer request:
' + JSON.stringify($('Paid Endpoint (x402 target)').item.json.body) + '

Current budget status:
' + JSON.stringify($('Budget Guard (deterministic)').item.json) }}

options.systemMessage:
You are the routing brain of a PAID research agent. The buyer paid a fixed price per call, so your job is to deliver quality while protecting margin.

Before deciding, call the UsageSummary analytics tool to read recent cost. Then pick a model tier:
- "premium" (model: gpt-4o) when recent margin is healthy or the request is high-value
- "economy" (model: gpt-4o-mini) when recent cost is high or the request is simple

Respond with STRICT JSON only, no prose, no code fences:
{"tier":"premium|economy","model":"gpt-4o|gpt-4o-mini","refinedPrompt":"<the prompt to run for the buyer>","reason":"<one short sentence>"}
